authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, representation, status, and semantics behavior that backend API guidance must align to.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract and protocol evidence review
practical_use_in_pack: protocol edge-case refusal when tenant policy conflicts with public semantics
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9457: Problem Details for HTTP APIs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9457 is the current standard for machine-readable HTTP API problem detail responses and updates the older RFC 7807 baseline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API error model and validation review
practical_use_in_pack: contract tests for consistent failure handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for HTTP API contract documents and reusable schemas.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract artifact assembly and review
practical_use_in_pack: schema compatibility and validation matrix design
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. national framework for secure software development practices, secure implementation evidence, and vulnerability response.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure development evidence review and remediation tracking
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST update, revision, or related federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized public verification standard for application security controls that backend services commonly need to satisfy.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backend security review and test planning
practical_use_in_pack: authentication, validation, session, data protection, and error-handling checks
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP ASVS release/version change.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP API Security Top 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 is a recognized public taxonomy of API-specific security risks for exposed backend services.
practical_use_in_pack: rate limit and resource risk review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OWASP API Security edition update.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenTelemetry Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTelemetry publishes vendor-neutral specifications for telemetry signals and instrumentation semantics used in backend observability workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: observability evidence review and incident follow-up
practical_use_in_pack: logs, metrics, traces, and instrumentation terminology
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on OpenTelemetry specification stability changes.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: About Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub's official documentation defines workflow evidence and CI behavior for repositories using GitHub Actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI evidence collection and quality-gate review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About Pull Request Reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub's official pull request review documentation defines review comments, requested changes, approvals, and review evidence in a common software delivery system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pull request review, finding status, and resubmission tracking
practical_use_in_pack: distinction between review support and approval authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Node.js API Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Node.js project publishes the official API reference for a common backend runtime used for HTTP services, diagnostics, process behavior, and error handling.
practical_use_in_pack: runtime failure analysis without privileged execution
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or on active LTS/current release changes that affect service guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: PostgreSQL Documentation: Transaction Isolation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The PostgreSQL project publishes authoritative database behavior documentation for transaction isolation in a common backend system of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: data consistency, migration, and concurrency review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the current PostgreSQL major version changes relevant isolation behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, privacy, confidentiality, harm avoidance, professional judgment, and duty boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, client delivery, and professional judgment boundary
practical_use_in_pack: refusal to overstate authority or expose tenant data
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ACM/IEEE Computer Society code is a recognized professional framework for software engineers, including public interest, client/employer obligations, product quality, judgment, and profession boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional-services quality, client commitment, and authority boundary
practical_use_in_pack: boundary between technical support and final approval
scenario coverage for client rights and professional judgment
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM/IEEE Computer Society code revision.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm backend task type, tenant facts, systems of record, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, source freshness, and expected output shape before assessing the request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: backend artifact and criteria review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect requirements, contracts, schemas, code, migrations, tests, logs, metrics, incidents, and review notes against backend correctness, reliability, security, maintainability, and protocol criteria.
source_classes: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: finding classification and recommendation drafting
workflow_stages.description: Convert evidence into implementation guidance, architecture notes, prioritized findings, validation matrix, or escalation package with clear assumptions, severity, owners, and confidence limits.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: remediation and resubmission tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track open findings, remediation owners, required tests, migration evidence, reviewer responses, and resubmission records without closing unsupported work.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: delivery, confidentiality, and signoff gating
workflow_stages.description: Before packaging a review-ready output, check recipient authorization, confidentiality, tenant policy, contractual scope, release approval, security ownership, and professional judgment boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: feature requirements, API/user stories, acceptance criteria, non-functional requirements, tenant policy, confidentiality terms, and approval matrix
service code, pull request diff, review comments, OpenAPI/protobuf/event contracts, schema registry entries, architecture diagrams, ADRs, and service catalog entries
database schema, migration history, transaction design, data ownership documentation, and rollback plan
bug reports, incident timelines, stack traces, logs, trace IDs, metrics, dashboards, SLO targets, and performance baselines
CI workflow definitions, build outputs, unit/integration/contract/security/performance test reports, release checklist, and artifact store records
review or inspection summary, evidence checklist, prioritized findings, validation matrix, remediation note, resubmission package, and escalation or refusal note
systems_of_record: source repository and pull request review system
API contract registry, schema registry, event catalog, or service catalog
issue tracker, incident tracker, remediation tracker, and review-note register
CI/CD system, test report store, build artifact store, and release checklist
observability platform for logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, and SLO evidence
database migration history and data model documentation
tenant document management system, engagement scope, confidentiality records, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend backend implementation plans, API contract fixes, transaction and migration patterns, review findings, validation matrices, and remediation steps
classify evidence as satisfied, unclear, failed, or blocked for backend review support
request missing artifacts and return low-confidence cases to orchestrator
route adjacent or authority-bound work to the correct owner
escalate: release approval, merge approval, contractual acceptance, legal interpretation, client rights, or licensed/professional judgment is required
tenant policy conflicts with public source guidance or confidentiality terms change the allowed output
critical repository, contract, CI, log, metric, incident, or approval evidence is missing or contradictory
security risk acceptance, platform operations, IAM tenancy, or network policy ownership is required
refuse: requests for frontend UI architecture, component design, visual systems, or client-side performance tuning
requests for Kubernetes cluster operations, IAM tenancy policy, firewall/routing changes, platform cost optimization, or privileged runtime execution
requests for product pricing, GTM strategy, legal/commercial contract work, attestation, or final signoff
requests to expose confidential tenant/client artifacts to unapproved recipients

domain_failure_modes: generic backend advice that is not tied to the provided requirements, contracts, code, tests, logs, or tenant systems of record
unsupported closure when review evidence, remediation tests, migration proof, or reviewer responses are missing
over-authoritative release, legal, contractual, security, or engineering signoff claims
confidential client or tenant data sent to an unapproved recipient or summarized beyond approved scope
security guidance that turns into security policy ownership or risk acceptance
platform, frontend, product, networking, or desktop endpoint work retained instead of routed
tenant-specific policy conflicts confidently resolved without approval owner review
runtime guidance assumes privileged credentials, secrets, production shell access, or tenant-specific facts not supplied

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
security and quality expectations where applicable
Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent.
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority.
client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack uses public backend sources and records that tenant-supplied runtime, framework, and policy details must be retrieved before stack-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, contractual, release, and licensed engineering authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to human owners or orchestrator.
